text_blocks.text: reached England in August. After several
months of negotiations, the government of
the Duke of Newcastle decided to send an
army expedition the following year to
dislodge the French. They chose Major
General Edward Braddock to lead the
expedition. Word of the British military
plans leaked to France well before
Braddock's departure for North America. In
response, King Louis XV dispatched six
regiments to New France under the
command of Baron Dieskau in 1755. The
British, intending to blockade French ports,
sent out their fleet in February 1755, but
the French fleet had already sailed. Admiral
Edward Hawke detached a fast squadron to
North America in an attempt to intercept
